pattern.summary: domain-specific strong decisions in concise expertise: Concise expertise should filter strong decisions that are clearly tied to a specific domain/tool stack (for example Playwright, SCIM, MPS, Keycloak, or E2E drive mechanics). Keep them available in full/debug mode unless rewritten as a general principle.

project-aware concise expertise: Concise expertise reads should suppress project-prefixed observations whose project does not match the current repo. Cross-project details belong in full/debug mode unless rewritten as a general principle.

expertise read defaults: Default expertise reads should present durable working memory, not changelog history. Keep complete history available through an explicit full/debug mode, and hide task-specific implementation breadcrumbs from the default LLM-facing output.

provider-scoped model blocklists: For aggressive /model cleanup requests, implement provider-scoped exact-ID and prefix blocklists inside a session_start model-visibility extension, layered on top of generic date/preview heuristics. This allows targeted hiding for openrouter catalogs while preserving modern keepers.

startup model pruning for noisy providers: A lightweight session_start extension can prune noisy model catalogs by re-registering target providers with filtered model lists. Current rule hides date/version-suffixed and preview snapshots (plus internal IDs) for github-copilot/opencode providers, improving /model UX without core TUI changes.

provider credential management command: A repo-local /provider command can safely manage credentials by writing through ctx.modelRegistry.authStorage.set/remove, which persists to ~/.pi/agent/auth.json and updates live session auth state. Include direct and interactive flows plus OAuth guidance to /login.

copilot model filtering in refresh-models: Copilot /models includes internal router IDs and hidden legacy entries. A robust refresh strategy is: parse only picker-enabled entries for newly discovered models, exclude internal accounts/* IDs, but preserve existing known models unless remote policy explicitly marks them disabled. This avoids polluting lists while not dropping known usable models like gpt-4.1/gpt-5.

refresh-models diff output UX: For /refresh-models, returning addedIds/removedIds from provider refresh and emitting follow-up notify lines (with capped ID list formatting) gives users actionable model-list diffs beyond count-only before/after summaries.

github-copilot /models refresh 400 handling: GitHub Copilot model discovery can return HTTP 400 'missing Editor-Version header for IDE auth' when provider models in registry lack Copilot IDE headers. /refresh-models should merge fallback headers from pi-ai built-in github-copilot model metadata before calling GET {baseUrl}/models, and include response-body snippets in errors for faster diagnosis.

pi custom slash command for subscription model refresh: A repo-local extension can register /refresh-models and use ctx.modelRegistry.authStorage + getApiKeyForProvider + registerProvider to refresh OAuth subscription availability without relogin. For each provider, fetch /models (provider-specific candidates), intersect ids with existing built-in provider models, then registerProvider with kept model defs and oauth provider callbacks.

observation.summary: {"task":"pi setup refactor and test failure cleanup","outcome":"Working tree is clean on main and origin/main at ed9f78d after commits through 4587f30/521ded2/bb5167f; make check-pi-extensions and make lint-python pass.","key_fixes":["ex...

Pi GPT-5.5 startup thinking level: pi/extensions/*.ts are auto-discovered. probe-thinking-level.ts previously mutated thinking level on session_start by setting minimal then xhigh, leaving sessions at xhigh. prompt-router.ts also had a GPT-5.5 special case that preserved current xhigh instead of lowering it. Fix pattern: keep session_start probes non-mutating, set pi/settings.json defaultThinkingLevel low, and avoid preserving xhigh for openai-codex/gpt-5.5.

Prompt router now has openai-codex/gpt-5.5-specific effort bias: medium is demoted to low unless classifier confidence >=0.8; high remains available; global max effort cap remains high, and manually-set xhigh is preserved when detected via pi.getThinkingLevel().

Pi auto-discovers every top-level `*.ts` in `~/.pi/agent/extensions/`; helper modules placed there must either move under a non-entry path or export a no-op default factory to avoid `Extension does not export a valid factory function`.

dotfiles: read_expertise LLM-facing output cleanup: read_expertise text should be distilled expertise only: no rebuild/similarity/layer/evidence metadata, no repeated category headings, and generic entries should summarize topic/details/discovery fields as prose instead of raw JSON. Diagnostics remain available in tool details and snapshot JSON.

dotfiles: read_expertise layered output compaction: pi/extensions/agent-chain.ts now formats multi-layer read_expertise results as a compact merged snapshot: one shared header, then layer sections in precedence order, with duplicate summaries deduped across all categories before lower-priority layers are printed. Raw fallback keeps the older concatenation path.

dotfiles: pi /context expertise bucket: pi/extensions/context.ts now breaks read_expertise usage out into an Expertise bucket by detecting assistant toolCall blocks with name read_expertise and toolResult messages with toolName read_expertise, excluding those tokens from generic Tool calls/Tool results.

dotfiles: pi /context output UX: pi/extensions/context.ts /context now emits a normal scrollback custom message (filtered from LLM context via context hook), removes Suggestions/Notes from report output, and aligns session spend values by padding value columns even without detail text.

dotfiles: Added a Pi extension command at pi/extensions/context.ts for Claude Code-style /context reporting. Pi exposes exact total context usage via ctx.getContextUsage(); component-level breakdown requires approximation from ctx.getSystemPrompt() and current session branch entries.

openrouter curated hide-list extension: Model-visibility extension now supports provider-scoped exact-ID and prefix blocklists, allowing iterative user-driven pruning of noisy OpenRouter catalogs (e.g., audio/search/legacy/vendor families) while retaining selected modern IDs.

codex hidden model filtering: OpenAI Codex /codex/models can include hidden internal entries (e.g., codex-auto-review with visibility: hide). Filtering parseOpenAICodexRemoteModels by visibility hide/hidden and supported_in_api=false prevents these from showing up in Pi model selection after refresh.

copilot model list contains non-usable entries: GitHub Copilot /models can include embeddings (text-embedding-*), internal router IDs (accounts/*), and synthetic oswe-* entries that are poor fits for Pi model selection. Refresh filtering now excludes those and keeps picker-enabled model entries (even when supported_endpoints is omitted), while still removing models explicitly marked policy.disabled.

anthropic subscription model discovery: Anthropic subscription tokens can be used to list models via GET https://api.anthropic.com/v1/models?limit=100 when sent as x-api-key with anthropic-version: 2023-06-01 (and Claude-Code beta headers optional). Using Authorization Bearer alone returns OAuth-not-supported errors on /v1/models.

refresh-models endpoint details: For OpenAI Codex subscriptions, live model discovery requires GET {baseUrl}/codex/models with a semver client_version query (high values like 999.0.0 work), plus Authorization bearer, originator, and chatgpt-account-id headers (account id extracted from JWT claim https://api.openai.com/auth.chatgpt_account_id). GitHub Copilot discovery works via GET {baseUrl}/models with bearer + Copilot headers; response includes policy/capability metadata usable for dynamic model defs.

pi model list updates: Pi built-in provider model catalogs are shipped with each release (docs/providers.md and README). User-level updates should use ~/.pi/agent/models.json for custom providers/models, modelOverrides for built-ins, and /model or pi --list-models to verify. /model reloads models.json when opened, so no restart is needed.

dotfiles: For pi agent caching research: installed pi docs show PI_CACHE_RETENTION=long enables extended prompt cache only for direct Anthropic/OpenAI API calls (Anthropic 1h, OpenAI 24h), while pi’s built-in context management remains AGENTS.md/SYSTEM.md loading plus auto-compaction. I found no package-level references to Gemini cachedContent/cached_content, suggesting no native explicit Gemini context-cache wiring in current pi package.
